name: Water & Waste Disposal Loan & Grant Program
description: Provides funding for clean and reliable drinking water systems, sanitary sewage disposal, sanitary solid waste disposal, and storm water drainage to households and businesses in eligible rural areas. This program assists qualified applicants who are not otherwise able to obtain commercial credit on reasonable terms. If funds are available, a grant may be combined with a loan to keep user costs reasonable.
eligibility: Eligible applicants include municipalities, counties, special-purpose districts, Indian tribes, and nonprofits. The project must serve a rural area with a population of 10,000 or less. Applicants must demonstrate they cannot obtain credit elsewhere at reasonable rates. Borrowers must have legal authority to construct, operate and maintain the proposed facilities.
funding_info: Loans and grants are available. Fixed interest rates based on the need for the project and the median household income of the area served. Grant amounts vary based on median household income of the service area.
how_to_apply: Applications are accepted year-round. Contact your local USDA Rural Development office or apply through RD Apply (the online application portal at rdapply.usda.gov). Working with a local RD specialist before applying is strongly recommended.
contact: Contact your local USDA Rural Development State Office. National contact: water-rd@usda.gov

name: Water & Waste Disposal Predevelopment Planning Grants
description: Assists low-income communities with initial planning and development of applications for USDA Rural Development Water and Waste Disposal direct loan/grant and loan guarantee programs. Helps communities conduct predevelopment feasibility studies, design and technical assistance on proposed water and waste disposal projects.
eligibility: Rural areas and towns with populations of 10,000 or less. The area must have a median household income below the poverty line or less than 80 percent of the statewide non-metropolitan median household income. Eligible applicants include municipalities, counties, districts, Indian tribes, and nonprofits.
funding_info: Maximum grant of $60,000 or 75 percent of the predevelopment planning costs, whichever is less. At least 25 percent of the project cost must come from the applicant or third-party sources. Grants do not have to be repaid if the subsequent water project application is unsuccessful.
how_to_apply: Applications are accepted year-round through your local RD office. Apply through RD Apply online system. Documents needed include proof of legal authority, financial statements, and public notice of intent to file.
contact: Contact your local USDA Rural Development State Office or email water-rd@usda.gov

name: Emergency Community Water Assistance Grants
description: Helps eligible communities prepare for or recover from an emergency that threatens the availability of safe, reliable drinking water. This program helps communities that have experienced a significant decline in quantity or quality of water, or are at risk of such a decline, due to events such as earthquakes, floods, tornadoes, contamination, or other disasters.
eligibility: Rural communities and towns with populations of 10,000 or less that have experienced or are at risk of a significant decline in water quantity or quality due to an emergency. Eligible applicants include municipalities, counties, special-purpose districts, Indian tribes, and nonprofits. The community must demonstrate the water problem is related to an emergency event.
funding_info: Grants up to $1,000,000 for construction projects. Grants up to $150,000 for predevelopment planning or repairs to existing water systems. No match required.
how_to_apply: Applications are accepted year-round. Contact your local USDA Rural Development office immediately after an emergency occurs. Priority is given to communities experiencing the most acute need.
contact: Contact your local USDA Rural Development State Office immediately. Find your state office at rd.usda.gov/contact-us/state-offices

name: Community Facilities Direct Loan & Grant Program
description: Provides affordable funding to develop essential community facilities in rural areas. Essential community facilities include schools, libraries, childcare centers, hospitals, medical clinics, assisted living facilities, fire and rescue stations, police stations, community centers, public buildings, and transportation infrastructure.
eligibility: Eligible applicants include municipalities, counties, special-purpose districts, Indian tribes, and nonprofits. The facility must be located in a rural area with a population of 20,000 or less. The project must be for a public use and serve the general public. Priority given to health care, education, and public safety.
funding_info: Below-market interest rate loans and grants available. Grant amounts determined by median household income and population of the area. Loans have 40-year terms. Grants can cover up to 75% of project cost for the most distressed communities.
how_to_apply: Applications accepted year-round. Contact your local USDA Rural Development office to discuss your project before applying. Apply online through RD Apply. An environmental review is required.
contact: Contact your local USDA Rural Development State Office. Find your state office at rd.usda.gov/contact-us/state-offices

name: Single Family Housing Direct Home Loans (Section 502)
description: Also known as the Section 502 Direct Loan Program. Helps low- and very-low-income applicants obtain decent, safe and sanitary housing in eligible rural areas by providing payment assistance to increase an applicant's repayment ability. Payment assistance is a type of subsidy that reduces the mortgage payment temporarily.
eligibility: Applicants must have low or very low income (below 80% of area median income). Must be without decent, safe and sanitary housing. Must be unable to obtain a loan from other sources on terms and conditions that can reasonably be expected to be met. Property must be located in an eligible rural area. U.S. citizen or eligible non-citizen.
funding_info: Loans subsidized to as low as 1% interest rate with payment assistance. 33-year loan term (38 years for very-low-income applicants). No down payment required. No private mortgage insurance required.
how_to_apply: Apply at your local USDA Rural Development office. Income and asset documentation required. Property must pass an inspection. Apply through RD Apply online or in person.
contact: Call 1-800-414-1226 or contact your local USDA Rural Development office.

name: Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program (Section 502)
description: Helps approved lenders provide low- and moderate-income households the opportunity to own adequate, modest, decent, safe and sanitary dwellings as their primary residence in eligible rural areas. The program provides a 90% loan note guarantee to approved lenders, which reduces lender risk and enables them to offer better terms.
eligibility: Applicants must have income at or below 115% of the area median income. Property must be in an eligible rural area. Must be a U.S. citizen or eligible non-citizen. Must have a reasonable credit history. The home must be the primary residence and meet certain property standards.
funding_info: No down payment required. Competitive fixed interest rates set by the approved lender. Loan guarantee fee of 1% upfront and 0.35% annual fee. Maximum loan amounts vary by location.
how_to_apply: Apply through any USDA-approved lender (banks, credit unions, mortgage companies). The lender submits the application to USDA on your behalf. Use the USDA eligibility site to check if your area qualifies.
contact: Find an approved lender through your local USDA RD office or at rd.usda.gov. Call 1-800-414-1226.

name: Business & Industry Loan Guarantees
description: Strengthens the existing private credit structure through the guarantee of quality loans which will provide lasting community benefits. This program bolsters the availability of private credit by guaranteeing loans for rural businesses. Helps create and save jobs and improve the economic and environmental climate in rural communities.
eligibility: Eligible businesses include for-profit and nonprofit businesses, cooperatives, federally-recognized tribes, public bodies, and individuals in rural areas (populations under 50,000, with priority for communities under 25,000). Loans cannot be used for projects that move jobs from urban to rural areas, or that increase production of goods with excess national supply.
funding_info: Loan guarantees up to $25 million. USDA guarantees up to 80% of the loan. Loan terms up to 30 years for real estate, 15 years for machinery and equipment, 7 years for working capital.
how_to_apply: Work with an eligible lender who will submit the application to USDA. Contact your local Rural Development office to find participating lenders and discuss eligibility before applying.
contact: Contact your local USDA Rural Development State Office.

name: Rural Energy for America Program (REAP)
description: Provides guaranteed loan financing and grant funding to agricultural producers and rural small businesses for renewable energy systems or to make energy efficiency improvements. Helps rural America become more energy efficient and develop and use renewable energy resources.
eligibility: Agricultural producers with at least 50% of gross income from agricultural operations, and rural small businesses located in eligible rural areas. Eligible projects include wind, solar, biomass, geothermal, hydropower, hydrogen, and energy efficiency improvements.
funding_info: Grants cover up to 50% of eligible project costs. Guaranteed loans available for the remaining project costs. Maximum grant of $1 million for renewable energy and $500,000 for energy efficiency. Combined grants and loan guarantees cannot exceed 75% of project cost.
how_to_apply: Applications accepted through your local USDA Rural Development office. Funding is competitive with application cycles published in the Federal Register. Contact your state office for current deadlines.
contact: Contact your local USDA Rural Development State Office. Find your state energy coordinator at rd.usda.gov.

name: ReConnect Program (Rural e-Connectivity)
description: Provides loans, grants, and loan-grant combinations to facilitate broadband deployment in rural areas. Helps rural communities across America obtain high-speed internet access. Broadband is critical for businesses, schools, healthcare, and economic development in rural areas.
eligibility: Eligible areas are rural areas where at least 90% of the households to be served do not have sufficient broadband access (currently defined as 100 Mbps download / 20 Mbps upload). Eligible applicants include for-profit businesses, nonprofits, states, local governments, tribes, and cooperatives.
funding_info: Grants up to $25 million. Loans up to $25 million. Loan-grant combinations available. 25% matching funds required for grants. Multiple funding rounds available annually.
how_to_apply: Applications accepted during open funding windows announced in the Federal Register. Contact your USDA Rural Development State Office for guidance before applying.
contact: Contact your local USDA Rural Development State Office or visit rd.usda.gov/reconnect

name: Rural Economic Development Loan & Grant Program (REDLG)
description: Provides funding to rural electric and telecommunications utilities to promote rural economic development and job creation projects. Zero-interest loans are made to local utilities which, in turn, pass the loans through to local businesses for projects that will create or retain employment in rural areas.
eligibility: Current or former Rural Utilities Service (RUS) electric or telecommunication borrowers, and not-for-profit utilities eligible under the Rural Electrification Act. The funded projects must create or retain employment in rural areas and be located in eligible rural communities.
funding_info: Zero-interest loans up to $2 million per project. Grants up to $300,000 available to establish revolving loan funds for lending to businesses. Quarterly funding competitions.
how_to_apply: Applications accepted quarterly. Deadlines: September 30, December 31, March 31, and June 30. Contact your local RD State Office to begin the application process. The preparation process can take weeks to months.
contact: Contact your local USDA Rural Development State Office.

name: Housing Preservation Grants
description: Provides grants to sponsoring organizations to assist very-low and low-income homeowners in repairing and rehabilitating their homes. Also assists rental property owners and cooperative housing complexes providing housing for very-low and low-income rural residents with the repair and rehabilitation of their units.
eligibility: Grants go to sponsoring organizations (states, local governments, nonprofits, federally recognized tribes) that then assist individual homeowners. Homeowners must have very low or low income (below 80% of area median income) and live in eligible rural areas. Renters in assisted buildings must also meet income requirements.
funding_info: Competitive grants to sponsoring organizations. Maximum grant of $3 million per recipient. Grant funds are passed through to assist eligible homeowners with repairs.
how_to_apply: Sponsoring organizations apply to USDA Rural Development. Funding is competitive. Contact your state Rural Development office for current application cycles.
contact: Contact your local USDA Rural Development State Office.

name: Community Facilities Technical Assistance & Training Grant
description: Provides grants to public bodies and nonprofits to assist rural communities in identifying and planning for community facility needs. Funding can be used to provide technical assistance and training for the development, maintenance, or expanding of essential community facilities in rural areas.
eligibility: Eligible applicants are nonprofits, most public bodies, and federally recognized tribes. The community facility must serve a rural area with a population of 20,000 or less. Must be for a public-use facility that primarily serves the general public.
funding_info: Grants up to $150,000. No matching funds required. Competitive funding.
how_to_apply: Applications accepted year-round at your local USDA Rural Development office. Apply through RD Apply online.
contact: Contact your local USDA Rural Development State Office.
